title: Coryphopteris japonica
taxonomy: Coryphopteris japonica is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to East Asia, specifically in China and Japan. The classification of Coryphopteris japonica includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Pteridophyta, and class as Polypodiopsida. This species belongs to the order Polypodiales and the family Thelypteridaceae. At the genus and species levels, its classification is Coryphopteris japonica.
uses: Coryphopteris japonica is utilized in traditional medicine for its purported benefits, including the treatment of fever and inflammatory conditions. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antiseptic and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, Coryphopteris japonica is used to ease respiratory issues and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis. Furthermore, the plant's leaves and stems are sometimes employed in folk remedies for their supposed antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Coryphopteris japonica for medicinal purposes.

intro: Coryphopteris japonica, also known as Japanese club fern or Tuberostachys japonica, is a type of fern plant native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. Bioactive constituents of Coryphopteris japonica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coryphopteris japonica is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote relaxation, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
